title: Claim Instruction
description:
is a Request submitted by a policyholder, beneficiary, or authorized party to begin the process of claiming benefits under a funeral policy arrangement

A claim instruction is a directive or request issued to the insurer (or internal claims processing team) specifying what action should be taken regarding a particular claim.

Key Points:

It tells the insurer or administrator how to process the claim.

May come from the policyholder, beneficiary, broker, or claims officer.

Examples:

Approve the funeral claim for Policy #12345.
Release partial payment for immediate expenses.
Investigate this claim before settlement.

Essentially, its the instruction that triggers the claim workflow.
